dishes.name: 2 Million Bees & Some Hops
dishes.raw_description: Our flagship New England style Double IPA brewed with a massive amount of orange blossom honey produced by two million hard working bees. Grounded in a foundation of our favorite English heritage malt, and intensively hopped, then double dry hopped, each glass greets you with pungent tropical fruit and hints of resinous pine, balanced by sweet robust malt, and a beautiful silky texture. Packed full of flavor, but deliciously drinkable, this is the perfect DIPA to create a buzz in any crowd.
dishes.reasoning: Contains orange blossom honey, an animal product. | evidence: “orange blossom honey”
dishes.address: 480 S Ronald Reagan Blvd Ste 1020, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: AESTHETICS // Peach Pomegranate
dishes.raw_description: Sip on the fruits of our labor and contemplate AESTHETICS. This heavily-fruited Florida-Weisse is brewed with massive amounts of plump peaches with a touch of tart pomegranate. Juicy pops of stone fruit explode from a light body that glows with a sparkling coral hue. The refreshing aromas and thirst-quenching flavors will make you pucker up, sip after sip. So raise your rose-colored glasses, because it's all in the AESTHETICS.
dishes.reasoning: Brewed with peaches and pomegranate; no animal products. | evidence: “peaches and pomegranate”

dishes.name: Apricaustic
dishes.raw_description: Proceed with apri-caution. Oozing with saccharine notes of floral honey and tart apricot, this extremely high gravity brew was made with a bouquet of hops, honey, and over two pounds per gallon of apricots. The slightly caustic bite at first sip is swiftly pacified with succulent notes of soothing honey. Don't let your taste buds fool you, this delightful drinker can sneak up with colossal vigor. 2020 Untappd - highest rated Honey Beer in the world

dishes.name: Batalla De Puebla
dishes.raw_description: Batalla de Puebla, uses heirloom ingredients to celebrate the bold flavors of Mexico. Bloody Butcher corn, and regionally, hand malted, craft Vienna malt from our friends at Riverbend Malthouse, combine to deliver a fragrant surge of biscuity malt and rich nuttiness. A dash of premium, single source, raw blue agave nectar contributes a quenching finish that begs for another sip. The result is a lager that is velvety smooth, shockingly crisp, and gently malty, with hints of freshly baked bread and soft baking spices imparted by the unique grain bill. Thanks to the indigenous artist, Obed 'OP' Vallejo for a truly remarkable label that pays homage to Cinco de Mayo and the resilience of the Mexican people.
dishes.reasoning: Uses corn, malt, and agave nectar (plant-based sweetener). | evidence: “raw blue agave nectar”
dishes.address: 480 S Ronald Reagan Blvd Ste 1020, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Double Dragon Paw
dishes.raw_description: Double Dragon Paw pays homage to its origin; an epic tale of two fruits that join together to battle the relentless swampy Florida heat. Brewed with double the fruit of its predecessor, this Florida Weisse style sour ale glows with formidable, fiery hot pink intensity. Vibrant notes of mango, pineapple, kiwi, watermelon, and banana bleed through the brew with a refreshingly tart, fruitful bite. Cool down with this juicy ale that's fit for a king, and enjoy the saga of the Double Dragon Paw.
dishes.reasoning: Fruit sour with mango, pineapple, etc.; no animal ingredients. | evidence: “mango, pineapple, kiwi, watermelon, and banana”

Jam-packed with luscious mango, juicy guava, and exotic dragonfruit purées, this non-alcoholic sour delivers a mouthwatering medley of sweetness and tartness. The bold fruit flavors shine through with a perfectly puckering finish, making it a refreshing go-to for any occasion. Whether you're poolside, relaxing with friends, or simply craving a tropical treat, this brew brings the vacation vibes — no passport (or alcohol) required.

dishes.name: Frankie Rock
dishes.raw_description: Conquerers of jagged cliffs and courageous climbs deserve nothing less than delicious, crushable brews. Frankie Rock is our sessionable take on a Bavarian-style Hefeweizen that radiates bountiful notes of ripe banana, spicy clove, and flourishes of sweet bubblegum. A roaring, frothy head sits atop a heavenly haze that roils its soft and crisp body. The silky mouthfeel soothes with a sigh of wheaty goodness and finishes with a gentle nudge to have another.

dishes.name: MikeChuck
dishes.raw_description: Who needs bait when you've got beer? An homage to one of our beloved patrons, MikeChuck is a beer best enjoyed while basking in the serene quietude of a lake, with a fishing pole in one hand and a beer in the other. This easy-drinking pale ale has wakatu and mandarin bavaria hops that add floral notes of mandarin orange and freshly zested lime. A balanced blend of malts complements the hops, creating a crisp, refreshing finish. Whether you end up with a bucket full of fish or just empty cans, MikeChuck is a good catch.
dishes.reasoning: Pale ale with hops and malts; no animal ingredients. | evidence: “wakatu and mandarin bavaria hops”

dishes.name: Raspberry Wheat
dishes.raw_description: Raspberry Wheat is a light ale brewed with pale wheat malt and fermented to a low terminal gravity to keep it super refreshing. Finally, we used hundreds of pounds of raspberry puree to give the beer the tart flavor of fresh raspberries.

dishes.name: Tripel Threat
dishes.raw_description: Adept in aroma, taste, and mouthfeel, this brew is a classic Tripel Threat! Expressive Belgian yeast infuses with a spiced malt backbone to form a robust, well-rounded body that's both highly carbonated and silky smooth. Scratch kitchen spices and sweet biscuit malts deliver a spirited bouquet bursting with notes of warm toffee and clove. Tripel Threat finishes dry with a pleasant warmth that is rivaled only by its own vibrant, golden glow.

dishes.name: Troy's Hammer
dishes.raw_description: Banished to Earth, a legend roams the terrain in search of a worthy immersion. In an effort to regain his godly status, he found a libation worthy of the gods. A black beer darkened with heavily kilned malts, Troy's Hammer balances roasted yet smooth malt flavors with a slight bitterness. A rich, bready character is accompanied by gentle notes of bitter chocolate and roasted coffee. Despite its menacing hue, this schwarzbier is light-bodied with a cleansing edge that leaves a crisp finish. Grasp the vindication, imbibe this venerated brew, and envisage his ascent as you pound Troy's Hammer.
dishes.reasoning: Schwarzbier with roasted malts; no animal products. | evidence: “heavily kilned malts”

dishes.name: Wolf Shirt Lager
dishes.raw_description: Wolf Shirt Lager is an American light lager - a clean and crisp 'beer flavored' beer. This beer is rumored to imbue the powers of the infamous Three Wolf Moon shirt. Hear the call, drink with caution. We used floor malted pilsner barley, flaked corn, and flaked rice to give the beer a light, refreshing flavor, body, and pale color. Just enough hops were added to provide balance and give the beer a refreshingly clean flavor and aroma.
dishes.reasoning: Grain bill includes barley, corn, rice; no animal ingredients. | evidence: “floor malted pilsner barley, flaked corn, and flaked rice”
